0: represents a shift from simply telling a computer what to do, to explaining why we need
1: something done and trusting it to figure out the how.
2: As exhilarating as this new frontier is, it brings a profound sense of responsibility, particularly
3: from my vantage point as the CIO of a global financial institution. The stakes are immeasurably
4: high. An agent that makes a mistake while creating a recipe for a "Chicken Salmon Fusion Pie"
5: is a fun anecdote. An agent that makes a mistake while executing a trade, managing risk, or
6: handling client data is a real problem. I've read the disclaimers and the cautionary tales: the
7: web automation agent that, after failing a login, decided to email a member of parliament to
8: complain about login walls. It's a darkly humorous reminder that we are dealing with a
10: This is where craft, culture, and a relentless focus on our principles become our essential guide.
11: Our Engineering Tenets are not just words on a page; they are our compass. We must Build
12: with Purpose, ensuring that every agent we design starts from a clear understanding of the
13: client problem we are solving. We must Look Around Corners, anticipating failure modes and
14: designing systems that are resilient by design. And above all, we must Inspire Trust, by being
15: transparent about our methods and accountable for our outcomes.
16: In an agentic world, these tenets take on new urgency. The hard truth is that you cannot simply
17: overlay these powerful new tools onto messy, inconsistent systems and expect good results.
18: Messy systems plus agents are a recipe for disaster. An AI trained on "garbage" data doesn't
19: just produce garbage-out; it produces plausible, confident garbage that can poison an entire
20: process. Therefore, our first and most critical task is to prepare the ground. We must invest in
21: clean data, consistent metadata, and well-defined APIs. We have to build the modern "interstate
22: system" that allows these agents to operate safely and at high velocity. It is the hard,
23: foundational work of building a programmable enterprise, an "enterprise as software," where our
24: processes are as well-architected as our code.
25: Ultimately, this journey is not about replacing human ingenuity, but about augmenting it. It
26: demands a new set of skills from all of us: the ability to explain a task with clarity, the wisdom to
27: delegate, and the diligence to verify the quality of the output. It requires us to be humble, to
28: acknowledge what we don't know, and to never stop learning. The pages that follow in this book
29: offer a technical map for building these new frameworks. My hope is that you will use them not
30: just to build what is possible, but to build what is right, what is robust, and what is responsible.
31: The world is asking every engineer to step up. I am confident we are ready for the challenge.
